## 3. Full FFA Check Declaration — Checks IAA Will Run at Handover

### 3a. Core Invariants (ALL-tagged — applicable to CI_WORKFLOW)

| Check ID | Check Name | What IAA Will Verify | Failure Outcome |
|----------|-----------|---------------------|----------------|
| CORE-005 | Governance block present | All delivered artifacts reference valid governance protocol, version, and canon_inventory paths. No placeholder values. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-006 | CANON_INVENTORY alignment | All governance artifacts cited in PR artifacts exist in `governance/CANON_INVENTORY.json` with non-null, non-placeholder SHA256 hashes | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-007 | No placeholder content | No "STUB", "TODO:", "FIXME:", "placeholder", "to be populated", "TBD" in any delivered artifact (workflow file, PREHANDOVER proof, session memory). Exception: `iaa_audit_token` pre-populated expected reference format is not a placeholder. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-013 | IAA invocation evidence | PREHANDOVER proof or IAA token reference present in PR artifacts. IAA audit token field must be non-empty. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-014 | No class exemption claim | Producing agent has not claimed any class exemption from IAA. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-015 | Session memory present | Session memory artifact included in PR bundle — file path must be present in PREHANDOVER proof artifact manifest. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-016 | IAA verdict evidenced (§4.3b) | Dedicated IAA token file exists at `.agent-admin/assurance/iaa-token-session-NNN-waveInjAudit-YYYYMMDD.md`. This is written by IAA post-verdict. At first-invocation time, PREHANDOVER `iaa_audit_token` must contain the expected reference format. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-018 | Complete evidence artifact sweep | All four evidence items present and non-empty before overlay checks: (a) PREHANDOVER proof file on branch, (b) session memory file on branch, (c) `iaa_audit_token` field non-empty and not a bare placeholder, (d) dedicated IAA token file exists (first-invocation carve-out applies — see CORE-019) | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-019 | IAA token cross-verification | If `iaa_audit_token` is a valid reference format: (a) verify token file exists, (b) verify it references this PR/branch, (c) verify verdict = ASSURANCE-TOKEN. **First-invocation exception**: if this is clearly the first IAA call for this session on this PR and no prior token file exists, CORE-019 = PASS (token file created this session). | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-020 | Zero partial pass rule | Any check that cannot be verified due to missing/blank/unverifiable evidence = REJECTION-PACKAGE. No assumed passes. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| CORE-021 | Zero-severity-tolerance | Any finding — regardless of perceived severity — produces REJECTION-PACKAGE. No "minor/trivial/cosmetic" soft-pass language. CS2 written waiver is the only exception. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |

### 3b. CI_WORKFLOW Overlay Checks (OVL-CI-001 through OVL-CI-005)

| Check ID | Check Name | What IAA Will Verify | Failure Outcome |
|----------|-----------|---------------------|----------------|
| OVL-CI-001 | Workflow policy correctness | Does the workflow actually implement what it claims? Does the scanning logic correctly identify the 9 injection marker strings? Does the acknowledgement detection logic correctly identify agent responses? Does the summary table posting logic work correctly? Does the idempotent comment mechanism (delete+repost) work as described? A workflow that inverts, mismaps, or silently skips its stated policy = REJECTION-PACKAGE. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| OVL-CI-002 | Merge gate integrity | All existing merge gate checks remain present and non-weakened after this PR. This workflow is additive — IAA will verify it does not interfere with or weaken any existing gate check. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| OVL-CI-003 | Silent failure risk | Are there unguarded `continue-on-error` uses? Are exit codes checked on all critical steps (API calls, comment posting, grep/count logic)? If any failure path would be swallowed silently, cite the specific line. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| OVL-CI-004 | Environment parity | Does the workflow behave consistently? Does it require `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` with correct scope? Are permissions declared explicitly? Any environment-dependent inconsistency must be stated and justified. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |
| OVL-CI-005 | CI evidence present | PREHANDOVER proof must include a CI check run URL or log snippet confirming the workflow executed successfully post-change. Claim without evidence = REJECTION-PACKAGE. If CI has not run yet at handover time, the producer must note this explicitly and provide evidence of a manual trigger run. | REJECTION-PACKAGE |

### 3c. FAIL-ONLY-ONCE Rules That Apply to This Wave

| Rule ID | Applies Because | What IAA Will Verify |
|---------|----------------|---------------------|
| A-001 | ALL PRs — invocation evidence | IAA invocation evidence must be present in PR artifacts |
| A-003 | Classification clarity check | N/A — classification is unambiguous CI_WORKFLOW. Recorded for completeness. |
| A-021 | Pre-commit gate | All artifacts must be committed and pushed BEFORE IAA is invoked. Working-tree-only files = REJECTION. |
| A-025 / A-029 | PREHANDOVER immutability | PREHANDOVER proof is read-only post-commit. `iaa_audit_token` must be pre-populated with expected reference, not left PENDING. |
| A-026 | SCOPE_DECLARATION match | `SCOPE_DECLARATION.md` must exactly match `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` at time of IAA invocation. |
| A-028 | SCOPE_DECLARATION format | List format required; prior-wave entries must be trimmed. |

## 4. Required PREHANDOVER Proof Structure

The producing agent (Copilot / foreman-v2-agent acting under CS2-Direct) **must** commit a PREHANDOVER proof before invoking IAA. The proof must contain the following sections, fully populated:

```markdown
# PREHANDOVER Proof — T-INJAUDIT-CI-001

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| task_id | T-INJAUDIT-CI-001 |
| wave | InjAudit |
| branch | copilot/create-injection-audit-report-workflow |
| issue | [CS2-Direct] New Workflow: injection-audit-report.yml |
| producing_agent | [agent name] |
| session_id | [session-InjAudit-YYYYMMDD] |
| date | [YYYY-MM-DD] |
| iaa_audit_token | IAA-session-InjAudit-waveInjAudit-YYYYMMDD-PASS |

## Scope Declaration
[Must match `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` exactly]

## Artifact Manifest
- `.github/workflows/injection-audit-report.yml` — NEW file (the deliverable)
- `[session memory file path]` — committed to branch
- `SCOPE_DECLARATION.md` — updated to match PR diff
- `.agent-admin/assurance/iaa-prebrief-InjAudit.md` — this Pre-Brief artifact

## CI Evidence (OVL-CI-005)
[CI check run URL or log snippet confirming workflow executed successfully]
OR
[Evidence of manual workflow_dispatch test run with output]

## Pre-IAA Commit Gate (A-021)
- `git status` output confirming clean working tree
- `git log --oneline -3` output confirming all artifacts committed
```

> **A-029 Compliance Note**: The `iaa_audit_token` field in the PREHANDOVER proof must be pre-populated with the expected reference format `IAA-session-InjAudit-waveInjAudit-YYYYMMDD-PASS` at commit time. IAA will write the dedicated token file **after** issuing its verdict. The PREHANDOVER proof is **READ-ONLY post-commit** — do not edit it after committing.

> **A-026 Compliance Note**: `SCOPE_DECLARATION.md` must be updated to exactly list all files changed in this PR. Run `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` and verify the output matches the scope declaration before committing.

## 5. Scope Blockers and Governance Conflicts

### 5a. Pre-Brief Scope Assessment

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Production code changes | NOT IN SCOPE | Wave task declares no production code changes — confirmed acceptable |
| Schema changes | NOT IN SCOPE | Wave task declares no schema changes — confirmed acceptable |
| UI changes | NOT IN SCOPE | Wave task declares no UI changes — confirmed acceptable |
| Agent contract changes | NOT IN SCOPE | Wave task declares no `.github/agents/` changes. IAA will verify via CORE-017 at handover. |
| Canon/governance changes | NOT IN SCOPE | Wave task is additive workflow only |
| AAWP/MAT deliverables | NOT IN SCOPE | No AAWP or MAT files in scope |

### 5b. Governance Conflicts — NONE DETECTED

No governance conflicts are visible at Pre-Brief time. Specific observations:

1. **Injection marker string fidelity** _(Advisory observation — not a blocker)_: The workflow must scan for the exact injection marker strings used by each source workflow (`agent-bootstrap-inject.yml`, `iaa-prebrief-inject.yml`, `iaa-prebrief-gate.yml`, `foreman-reanchor.yml`, `governance-watchdog.yml`, `copilot-push-intercept.yml`). IAA will verify at handover (OVL-CI-001) that the 9 marker strings embedded in the audit workflow match the actual injection strings fired by those workflows. Mismatch = silent miss = OVL-CI-001 FAIL.

2. **Permissions scope** _(Advisory observation)_: The workflow posts a PR comment and reads PR comments — it requires `pull-requests: write` and `pull-requests: read` (or equivalent) permissions in the YAML. Missing permissions = OVL-CI-004 concern. Ensure the `permissions:` block is explicit.

3. **Idempotent comment mechanism** _(Advisory observation)_: If the workflow uses a delete-and-repost strategy to prevent duplicate summary comments, the delete step must handle the case where no prior comment exists (first run) without silently failing. This will be checked under OVL-CI-003.

4. **`workflow_dispatch` input schema** _(Advisory observation)_: If `workflow_dispatch` supports optional inputs (e.g., `pr_number`), validate that missing inputs are handled gracefully. Unhandled null inputs = silent failure risk (OVL-CI-003).

5. **No SCOPE_DECLARATION.md visible yet** _(Information)_: At Pre-Brief time, the producing agent has not yet delivered the workflow. `SCOPE_DECLARATION.md` update is required before IAA invocation (A-026 / A-028).

### 5c. CS2-Direct Authority Confirmation

This wave was opened directly by CS2 (@APGI-cmy) as a CS2-Direct task assigned to Copilot. CS2-Direct authority is confirmed in `wave-current-tasks.md`. No additional authorization artifact required — the wave task file is the authorization record.
